title: Clamp invalid day-of-month in parse_due
author: alice
body: Partial fix for #1 and #3. Clamps feb 29 -> feb 28 in non-leap years inside parse_due.
diff:
@@ parse_due @@
         month = _MONTHS.get(month_name)
         if month is None:
             raise ParseError(f"unknown month: {month_name}")
-        try:
-            return dt.date(today.year, month, day)
-        except ValueError as e:
-            raise ParseError(str(e)) from e
+        import calendar
+        last = calendar.monthrange(today.year, month)[1]
+        return dt.date(today.year, month, min(day, last))
review_targets: This fixes parsing but does NOT address #1's recurrence-pass abort: roll_recurrences still has no per-task error containment, so any other un-rollable task still kills the whole pass.
No regression test was added. tests/core/test_recurrence.py::test_yearly_leap_rolls_without_crash and tests/dates/test_parsing.py::test_leap_year_clamping should be made to pass.
Importing calendar inside the function on every call is wasteful — hoist to module scope.
